raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.3,"R1.3":0.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.6":"\"Clean this up\" most naturally denotes prose-level work — sentence clarity, concision, and paragraph cohesion — making writing polish the single load-bearing responsibility under the most probable reading."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should edits be limited to prose quality and readability only, or should they also address substantive conceptual and logical issues — for example, the disconnect between the paper's Lyapunov-adaptive framing (claiming convergence guarantees and automatic compensation under non-stationary distributions) and the actual implementation (a static binary classifier with a fixed threshold that is explicitly never updated at inference time)?"}

rationale.R1.6: "Clean this up" most naturally denotes prose-level work — sentence clarity, concision, and paragraph cohesion — making writing polish the single load-bearing responsibility under the most probable reading.

parsed.clarification_question: Should edits be limited to prose quality and readability only, or should they also address substantive conceptual and logical issues — for example, the disconnect between the paper's Lyapunov-adaptive framing (claiming convergence guarantees and automatic compensation under non-stationary distributions) and the actual implementation (a static binary classifier with a fixed threshold that is explicitly never updated at inference time)?
